spec_match.rationale: The toolbar uses the current behavior-first syntax focusgroup="toolbar wrap", matching ChromeStatus and the scoped Focusgroup explainer.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified ArrowRight, ArrowLeft, wrap from first to last, End, and Tab exit from the toolbar into the editor.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The visible copy and code sample describe toolbar, block, wrap, and nomemory rather than obsolete axis-only token values.

edge_cases.rationale: MCP verified wrap behavior and Tab exit, while the page still documents the unsupported-browser fallback.

references.rationale: The page cites the ChromeStatus entry, the current scoped Open UI explainer, and the Microsoft Edge focusgroup demos.

design_system.rationale: The page keeps the shared CSS variables, focus rings, contrast-safe button states, and no custom raw color additions.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP verification used Chrome Canary 151 with experimental web platform features enabled. Toolbar arrow movement, wrapping, Tab exit, console, network, and conformance checks passed.
